We Acer India Pvt. Ltd, one of the largest OEM of Desktop, Laptop, Server, TFT etc, having 
registered office at Embassy Heights, 6th Floor, No 13 Magrath Road, Bangalore 560025 and

FAQ on CID for Service provider
 
Q1. What is CID?
Ans.    Customer Induced Defect/Damage observed due to PCB Track cut or scratched/ PCB
Broken/Component missing/ CPU socket pin bent/ PCB or Component Burnt/ Serial Number Label
tempered.
Q2. Which all cases Qualify for CID?
Ans.    All the above mentioned cases will qualify for CID.
Q3. Does a CID case qualify for a warranty replacement?
Ans.    CID is considered as customer induced and is not covered by any standard warranty. Therefore, it
will be the Customer's responsibility to cover the cost of spare unless they have prior approval from the
AIL Customer Support Manager.

Q4. At what stage CID case is identified by Acer & what would be the action?
Ans.    There will be 2 stages, 1) physical verification of a defective part for visual damage when received
at Warehouse, while making a receiver in System & 2) At the Repair Centre in Dhaka, which will primarily
highlight cases of component missing, burnt and track cut. AIL will bill such CID Spares to respective
Service provider as per AIL price list with additional cost USD 5 towards logistics. CID spares returned to
Service along with invoice. In case of any exception/ waiver - the necessary mail approval from the
Customer Support Manager needs to be provided within 3 BDs from the date of intimation.
Q5. If in the rare case, part received from Acer is found to be Damaged, what needs to be done?
Ans.    Service provider has to physically verify each & every part received from Acer and if any part found
to be damaged, same needs to be reported to Acer Branch Customer Support Manager with in 24 Hrs
(Next working day) of receipt by an Email, failing which if service provider returns the parts as unused /
received DOA after a period & if its identified as CID same will attract Penalty.
Q6. What should be done to avoid damages in transit which results as CID ?
Ans.    Service Provider should return the defective part in the same box against which the good spare
was received from Acer . Packing plays a very vital role in avoiding transit damage which will be seen as
CID case, if due care is not taken. In case Partner feels the quality of the box/packing is not up to the
mark, they should inform Acer for sending the packing material/Box.
Q7. In the event of attending a call, Engineer finds the customer part to be CID, what should
   be done?
Ans.    A)  Customer needs to be informed on the spot. Usually burnt case of Adapters, SMPS
           , M/b and TFT monitors etc. are due to the site conditions which are not suitable for proper
           functioning of the electronic devices.
           In case of doubt, must speak to the Branch CSM/ Regional Technical Support Manager by
           providing the Snap shot with the Case ID & FRU SL#.
